note: Spec-owned vectors. Each vector is recomputable from `inputs` alone; `expected` is the outcome a correct reviewer must reach FROM the inputs. A reference impl imports nothing from this bench and recomputes from inputs. The bench scores reviewability of evidence PER AXIS, with NO aggregate score.

axes.sufficiency: a valid record is not sufficient without coverage
axes.source_class_ceiling: who attested caps the maximum supportable claim
axes.recompute: the verdict is recomputed from records, not asserted; prose is ignored
axes.format_equivalence: shape-different but semantically-same records group as equivalent
axes.tamper_fail_closed: any digest mismatch or missing digest fails closed
axes.incomplete_visibility: absent / not-checked observation surfaces as incomplete, never clean
axes.coverage_honesty: a declared expected set plus retained per-case results bounds confirmation; absent or unresolved cases are incomplete, explicit failures may refute
axes.delegated_scope: a delegation grant caps the used scope; authorization narrows, never widens
axes.hard_soft_digest: hard integrity is the gate; the soft equivalence digest sits beside it and never rescues a hard failure
axes.retained_replay: the verdict is the recompute over retained records, not the carrier; a carrier over absent records is incomplete
axes.mcp_description_code: a tool's declared interface is checked against its code-evident effects; prose is ignored

vectors.property: valid record + full coverage is sufficient
vectors.non_claims: does not assert the action occurred, only that the evidence supports a conclusion

vectors.property: valid record with no coverage is NOT sufficient (absent != clean)
vectors.non_claims: incomplete is not a failure of the record, it is a gap in coverage

vectors.property: an invalid record cannot be sufficient regardless of coverage
vectors.non_claims: validity is necessary but not sufficient

vectors.property: a self-report may carry an asserted-level claim
vectors.non_claims: within-ceiling is not proof the action occurred

vectors.property: a self-report cannot reach independently_confirmed
vectors.non_claims: the claim is capped, not the record rejected

vectors.property: a third-party observation may carry independently_confirmed
vectors.non_claims: ceiling ordering is RGE-Bench's proposed ranking, not a standard

vectors.property: observed effects within declared -> match
vectors.non_claims: static/record-level, not runtime behavior

vectors.property: an observed effect outside declared -> mismatch
vectors.non_claims: mismatch is an evidence signal, not a verdict on intent

vectors.property: prose is not evidence: rewording the description does not change the verdict
vectors.non_claims: the recompute ignores the natural-language description

vectors.property: same envelope shape + same semantics -> equivalent
vectors.non_claims: equivalence is over semantic fields, not raw bytes

vectors.property: DIFFERENT envelope shapes + same semantics -> still equivalent (cross-shape normalization)
vectors.non_claims: the envelope shape is metadata, excluded from equivalence

vectors.property: same shape + different semantics -> distinct
vectors.non_claims: a real semantic difference must split

vectors.property: stored digest equals recomputed -> accepted
vectors.non_claims: acceptance is integrity only, not authenticity

vectors.property: any digest mismatch -> rejected (fail closed)
vectors.non_claims: rejection is integrity-only, not an attribution claim

vectors.property: missing stored digest -> rejected (fail closed, never degrade to a soft pass)
vectors.non_claims: absence of a digest is not a pass

vectors.property: an observed surface -> observed
vectors.non_claims: observed means watched, not that the result is good

vectors.property: a not-checked surface surfaces as incomplete, never clean
vectors.non_claims: not_checked is a visible gap, not a pass

vectors.property: an absent observation surfaces as incomplete
vectors.non_claims: absent != clean

vectors.property: every declared case produced a passing retained result -> confirmed
vectors.non_claims: confirmed is bounded to the declared case set, not a general truth claim

vectors.property: a declared case with no retained result blocks confirmation (absent != pass)
vectors.non_claims: not_run is a coverage gap, not a failure of the passing cases

vectors.property: errored or missing-result cases block confirmation without refuting
vectors.non_claims: incomplete names the unresolved coverage boundary, not a soft maybe

vectors.property: an explicit failed case may refute even when other declared cases are unresolved
vectors.non_claims: a clear contradiction can refute; partial evidence still cannot confirm

vectors.property: without a declared expected set, coverage cannot be evaluated
vectors.non_claims: invalid is shape failure, not a failed evaluation

vectors.property: coding agent: observed file/command/tool effects all within declared scope -> match

inputs.description: Edited the target module and ran the tests.

vectors.non_claims: record-level scope check, not a runtime-behavior guarantee

vectors.property: coding agent: an edit to a file outside declared scope -> mismatch
vectors.non_claims: mismatch is an evidence signal, not a verdict on intent

vectors.property: coding agent: an MCP tool call outside declared scope -> mismatch
vectors.non_claims: the tool surface is part of the declared scope

vectors.property: coding agent: an authorized run whose observed effect exceeds scope still -> mismatch (authorization does not upgrade)

inputs.description: Authorized refactor of the target module.

vectors.non_claims: a delegation authorizes; observation confirms -- authorization is not conformance

vectors.property: coding agent: the agent's own log (producer_reported) cannot reach independently_confirmed
vectors.non_claims: the claim is capped, not the record rejected

vectors.property: coding agent: a boundary-observed run may carry observed_in_path
vectors.non_claims: ceiling ordering is RGE-Bench's proposed ranking, not a standard

vectors.property: coding agent: an evidence pack whose body was altered after hashing -> rejected
vectors.non_claims: rejection is integrity-only, not an attribution claim

vectors.property: coding agent: a pack with no content hash -> rejected (fail closed)
vectors.non_claims: absence of a content hash is not a pass

vectors.property: coding agent: the network surface was not observed -> incomplete (absent != clean)
vectors.non_claims: network egress is high-blast-radius; an unobserved surface is a gap, not a pass

vectors.property: coding agent: the shell-command surface was not checked -> incomplete
vectors.non_claims: not_checked is a visible gap, not a pass

vectors.property: coding agent: the MCP-tool surface was observed -> observed
vectors.non_claims: observed means the surface was watched, not that the result is good

vectors.property: coding agent: a valid pack with all core surfaces observed is sufficient
vectors.non_claims: sufficient supports a conclusion; it does not assert the change is correct

vectors.property: coding agent: a valid pack missing network coverage is NOT sufficient
vectors.non_claims: incomplete is a coverage gap, not a defect in the record

vectors.property: coding agent: a malformed pack (e.g. missing network policy) cannot be sufficient even with full coverage
vectors.non_claims: validity is necessary but not sufficient

vectors.property: the used scope is a subset of the granted scope -> within_grant
vectors.non_claims: within_grant means the used scope is covered by the grant, not that the action occurred or was correct

vectors.property: a used capability outside the grant -> exceeds_grant
vectors.non_claims: exceeds_grant is an authorization signal, not a verdict on intent

vectors.property: an empty grant authorizes nothing; any use exceeds it (absent != permission, fail closed)
vectors.non_claims: an absent grant is not an open grant

vectors.property: a missing granted field cannot be evaluated -> invalid (never default to permit)
vectors.non_claims: invalid is unevaluable, distinct from a denied use

vectors.property: coding agent: a sub-delegate using a subset of the parent grant -> within_grant
vectors.non_claims: a sub-delegation may narrow the grant

vectors.property: coding agent: a sub-delegate adding a capability beyond the parent grant -> exceeds_grant
vectors.non_claims: a sub-delegation cannot widen the grant it descends from

vectors.property: hard integrity intact and the soft projection matches -> soft_equivalent
vectors.non_claims: soft equivalence is advisory grouping, not an integrity or authenticity claim

vectors.property: hard integrity intact but the soft projections differ -> soft_divergent
vectors.non_claims: soft divergence flags a semantic difference for review, it is not a failure of integrity

vectors.property: hard integrity fails -> rejected_hard even when the soft projection would match (soft never rescues hard)
vectors.non_claims: a matching soft digest never upgrades or overrides a hard-integrity failure

vectors.property: a missing hard digest fails closed -> rejected_hard (never degrade to a soft pass)
vectors.non_claims: absence of a hard digest is not a pass

vectors.property: coding agent: a pack whose bytes were altered (hard fail) but whose semantic projection is unchanged -> rejected_hard
vectors.non_claims: an unchanged semantic projection does not make a tampered pack acceptable

vectors.property: retained records replayed reproduce the recorded effect -> replayed_match
vectors.non_claims: match is over the retained records, not a runtime-behavior guarantee

vectors.property: retained records replayed diverge from the recorded effect -> replayed_mismatch
vectors.non_claims: mismatch is an evidence signal, not a verdict on intent

vectors.property: a valid carrier over absent retained records -> incomplete (carrier != verdict)
vectors.non_claims: a valid carrier does not manufacture a verdict; without retained records the conclusion is incomplete

vectors.property: an invalid carrier cannot support replay even when its records would match -> rejected_carrier
vectors.non_claims: carrier validity is a precondition to trust the retained records, not the verdict; an invalid carrier fails closed before any replay is trusted

vectors.property: coding agent: a retained evidence pack replayed offline reproduces the recorded effect -> replayed_match
vectors.non_claims: the verdict is the offline recompute, not the pack's signature

vectors.property: coding agent: a signed pack with its effect records stripped -> incomplete (signature is carrier, not verdict)
vectors.non_claims: a valid signature over absent records is incomplete, never a clean pass

vectors.property: code-evident effects equal the declared interface -> consistent
vectors.non_claims: consistent is a declared-vs-code check, not a runtime-behavior guarantee

vectors.property: code does more than the interface declares (hidden capability) -> undeclared_effect
vectors.non_claims: undeclared_effect is an inconsistency signal, not a verdict on intent

vectors.property: the interface declares an effect the code never exercises -> over_declared
vectors.non_claims: over_declared is a description-code mismatch, not by itself a security finding

vectors.property: when the code both adds an undeclared effect AND the interface over-declares another, undeclared_effect takes precedence (pinned)
vectors.non_claims: the precedence is pinned so a second implementation cannot reasonably return over_declared; a hidden (code-does-more) capability is the higher-blast-radius signal and is reported first

vectors.property: rewording the description does not change the verdict; prose is not evidence
vectors.non_claims: the check ignores the natural-language description

vectors.property: coding agent: a tool declaring only file read whose code opens a network socket -> undeclared_effect
vectors.non_claims: the tool surface is part of the declared interface; an undeclared socket is an inconsistency signal
